channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Sanwo-Olu pays N3.1bn compensation to 300 Lagos property owners

item.description:

item.title: Drake loses $1.5m world cup bet as Spain beat Argentina
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Canadian rapper Drake loses a $1.5 million bet after Argentina’s defeat to Spain in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final, fueling the ‘Drake curse’ theory.
Read More: https://punchng.com/drake-loses-1-5m-world-cup-bet-as-spain-beat-argentina/

item.description: George Russell says he has been "knocked down" but will "get back up" after the latest setbacks to his world championship challenge at the Belgian Grand Prix

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sky Sports takes a look back at the incredible career of former England manager and legendary footballer Kevin Keegan, who has died at the age of 75.

item.title: Miyetti Allah Leader Secures N2bn Bail In Money Laundering Trial
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has granted bail to the National President of Miyetti Allah Kautal Hore, Bello Abdullahi Bodejo, in the sum of N2bn as he faces trial over alleged $2.33 million money laundering charges filed by the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC). Justice I.E. Ekwo, w

item.title: First HoldCo Posts N526.13bn H1 Profit As Interest Income Surges
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Assets Hit N30.65trn First HoldCo Plc delivered a strong financial performance for the first half of 2026, posting a profit after tax of N526.13 billion, representing an 81.6 per cent increase from N289.77 billion recorded in the corresponding period of 2025, driven by higher interest income,

item.title: 2026 Ballon d’Or: Voting dates for nominees confirmed as World Cup ends
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: This year’s Ballon d’Or ceremony will take place on October 2026. It will be decided based on the results of the period running from August 1, 2025, through to 31 July, 2026. There are already conversations over who the possible nominees could be, after the World Cup ended on Sunday n

item.title: Sanwo-Olu Disburses Over N3bn To 331 Landowners Affected By Infrastructure Projects
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Lagos State Government has disbursed more than ₦3.1 billion in compensation to over 300 landowners whose properties were acquired for public infrastructure projects across the state. Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu presented the compensation cheques to beneficiaries at an event held at the Secretari

item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Annie Macaulay urged fans not to believe unverified social media claims. The post ‘Old videos, false news’ — Annie Macaulay denies reuniting with 2Baba appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: World Cup 2026: How I Lost ₦5m Bet On Argentina –Popular Nollywood Actress
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nollywood actress Destiny Etiko has lost ₦5 million after placing her very first football bet on the 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain. Following Argentina’s 1-0 defeat, she took to Instagram to lament the loss, stating that the anxiety was overwhelming and vowing never to

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Union of Textile, Garment and Tailoring Workers of Nigeria (NUTGTWN) has dissolved its Edo State Executive Committee and suspended all union activities in the state indefinitely over alleged insubordination and disregard for directives from the union’s national leadership. The dec

item.title: Mediators push 10-day ceasefire to revive US-Iran memorandum
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: International mediators have presented Iran with a proposal for a 10-day ceasefire aimed at reducing tensions with the United States and reopening negotiations on an interim agreement reached last month, according to a senior Iranian official. The proposed truce is intended to create diplomatic spac

item.description: The FDA and CDC are investigating iceberg lettuce sold by Taylor Farms to confirm the source of the cyclospora outbreak

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Delta State Police Command has foiled a suspected cult initiation ceremony and recovered firearms, ammunition, seven vehicles and other exhibits during an intelligence-led operation in Orogun Community, Ughelli North Local Government Area. The post Delta police foil suspected cult initiation, re

item.title: FIFA Rankings: Super Eagles remain 26th, slip to fourth in Africa
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria's Super Eagles have retained 26th place in the latest FIFA men's world rankings but dropped to fourth in Africa following Monday's update. The post FIFA Rankings: Super Eagles remain 26th, slip to fourth in Africa appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: The Whistler

Speaking at the presentation ceremony in Lagos, Sanwo-Olu said the c

The singer made the assertion during an appearance on Rubbin’ Minds on Channels Television, where he described himself as a major force in

item.title: Spain Receive Hero’s Welcome After World Cup Triumph
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Spain‘s World Cup-winning squad returned to Madrid on Monday to a heroes’ welcome as thousands of fans gathered to celebrate the nation’s second FIFA World Cup title ahead of a victory parade through the capital. La Roja were crowned world champions after Ferran Torres scored the d

item.title: Stop importing poverty, Atiku tells FG, advocates massive industrialization
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Omeiza Ajayi ABUJA: Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress ADC, Atiku Abubakar, has told the Federal Government that it cannot borrow, import and tax its way to prosperity, insisting that the country’s economic salvation lies in producing what it eats, manufacturing what

item.title: Hackers stole ‘significant’ amount of data from tech firm relied on by thousands of US hospitals and pharmacies
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Edinburgh-based tech firm Craneware said customer data was stolen during a cyberattack. The company makes software that thousands of U.S. hospitals, pharmacies, and clinics rely on for billing patients, potentially exposing health data.

item.description: The positivity and vibrancy Kevin Keegan brought made him a cherished figure within the game, writes chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Burnham to Seek ‘Flexibility’ Within Existing Fiscal Rules
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham indicated he’d look at using what he called “flexibility” within the UK’s existing fiscal rules to fund his next budget.

item.title: Dr. Jill Lepore on why AI backlash is vital for the future
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Harvard professor and New Yorker staff writer Dr. Jill Lepore about her new book, The Rise and Fall of the Artificial State, which comes out on August 25. Jill is one of the best writers there is at identifying institutional patterns in history, and Decoder is a show about sy

item.title: What does Rogers' move mean for Chelsea's squad?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Morgan Rogers is set to join Chelsea in a British-record £117m from Aston Villa - and his arrival is set to have a knock-on effect for Xabi Alonso's Blues.

The organisation, while condemn

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: One hundred and twenty-seven new staff of Michael Okpara University of Agriculture,, Umudike, Abia State, who were employed by the Federal Government , have appealed to President Bola Tinubu to prevail on the management of the institution to reinstate them as duly employed workers of the institution

item.title: Nigeria’s pension industry consolidates as N31 trillion asset base fuels mergers
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria’s pension industry is undergoing a major transformation as mergers and acquisitions reshape the competitive landscape, creating larger and stronger Pension Fund Administrators (PFAs) in a market now managing over N31 trillion in assets. The post Nigeria’s pension industry consolidates as N31

item.title: 10 countries in Europe that give visas easily to Africans
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: In recent years, an increasing number of Africans have sought opportunities in Europe through study, work, business and long-term residence programmes. While many are pursuing higher education, skilled employment or entrepreneurship, others are looking to build new lives through legal immigration pa

item.title: Economic hardship spurs contraceptive acceptance in Jigawa
item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: IN Jigawa State, northwest Nigeria, economic hardship is forcing many couples to reconsider tradition. Across the conservative northern region, where family planning is often viewed as controversial, this ICIR special report explores why more couples are now choosing to have fewer children despite l

item.title: Key things Commonwealth citizens should know about UK visas
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: If you have ever applied for an international scholarship or registered for a global training programme, you have probably come across the word "Commonwealth." The post Key things Commonwealth citizens should know about UK visas appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: A protest broke out in Gero community, Jos South Local Government Area of Plateau State, on Monday after two women were allegedly killed by suspected gunmen, with angry residents vandalising parts of the local government secretariat and reportedly attacking a police officer. Danburam Peter a residen

item.title: Malawi: Malawi Weighs Tax Breaks for Fish Farmers As Wild Stocks Dwindle
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nyasa Times] Malawi's government is considering tax and duty waivers for investors in fish cage farming, as it looks to aquaculture to offset declining catches from Lake Malawi and other natural water bodies.

item.description: Chelsea's £117m move for Aston Villa forward Morgan Rogers is a genuine transfer window shock, but how did they do it and what does it mean for the rest of the squad?

item.title: Destiny Etiko Says She’s Done With Football After N5 Million Betting Loss
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nollywood actress Destiny Etiko says she has no plans to return to professional football after losing N5 million on a bet placed on the 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina. The actress shared her frustration in an Instagram post on Sunday night after Spain edged Argentina to lift t

item.title: Nigeria hails ECOWAS backing for African Atlantic Gas Pipeline
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Nigeria has welcomed ECOWAS’ endorsement of the African Atlantic Gas Pipeline Project, describing the signing of the intergovernmental agreement as a major step towards regional energy security, economic integration and industrial development. The post Nigeria hails ECOWAS backing for African Atlant

item.title: Lagos unveils N10m single-digit loan scheme for MSMEs
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the commissioner, LASMECO addresses the challenge by using registered cooperative societies as financial intermediaries and guarantors, allowing entrepreneurs to obtain loans without relying solely on conventional collateral.

item.title: ‎‎‎“Na Small Pikin Go Bath You When You Old” – Ayo Makun Reacts To Viral Video Of Messi Crying For Losing The World Cup
item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: Essential AI Skills Every African Entrepreneur Needs to Succeed
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: | By: Tony Ajah A few years ago, the biggest threat to a small business owner in Lagos, Nairobi, or Accra was a better-funded competitor down the road. Today, the threat and the opportunity sit inside a laptop screen. AI has quietly rewritten the rules of who wins in business, and the entrepren

item.title: Passenger Left Shocked After Allegedly Spotting Commercial Driver Asleep Behind The Wheel
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A passenger aboard a commercial vehicle was left stunned after allegedly discovering that the driver appeared to be asleep while the vehicle was still in motion. A video circulating on social media shows the driver with his eyes closed as the vehicle continues along the road, prompting concern among

item.title: Cardi B And Super Eagles Goalkeeper Maduka Okoye Seen Together Again In Italy
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: American rapper Cardi B and Super Eagles goalkeeper Maduka Okoye have once again been seen together, this time during a waterfront dinner in Venice, Italy, days after they attended Paris Fashion Week events. The pair were spotted seated side by side at an upscale restaurant overlooking one of Venice

item.title: Andy Burnham pledges end to political instability as he becomes UK PM
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Britain's seventh PM in a decade pledged major changes in bid to see off populist forces that are making large gains.

item.title: Has The World Cup Turned Tactical Analysis Into Mainstream Online Entertainment?
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Football has always been a popular topic of conversation, but now people are able to discuss it beyond a surface level. Thanks to the increase in football analysts on platforms like YouTube, fans can dive into much greater detail about what they’ve just seen on the pitch. The latest World Cup has le

item.title: EFCC arraigns ex-Warri refinery MD for money laundering
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The EFCC alleged that Mr Yisawu laundered proceeds of unlawful activity while serving as managing director of the refinery and used part of the funds to purchase treasury bills. The post EFCC arraigns ex-Warri refinery MD for money laundering appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: WTA Introduces Mandatory Sex Testing For Women’s Tour
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Women’s Tennis Association (WTA) is to require all players to undergo genetic sex testing in order to compete on the WTA Tour. In the latest edition of its women’s eligibility policy, which will come into effect on Tuesday, players will be required to provide a cheek swab, a blood sa

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: As the world celebrated the conclusion of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, a group of Palestinian amputee footballers staged a symbolic final in a war-damaged stadium in western Gaza City to draw global attention to the humanitarian crisis unfolding in the enclave. The players, many of whom lost limbs durin

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph

The Board of Directors approved an interim dividend of N1.00 per o

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph

This was even as it also targets its Grassroots Digital Extension Corps (GDEC) initiative ac

item.title: Netflix Returns to the High-Grade Bond Market After 2024 Debut
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Netflix Inc. is tapping the US high-grade bond market for the first time since its debut deal two years ago, as slowing sales growth fuels investor scrutiny of the company’s future.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.description: Kevin Keegan scores twice as Liverpool defeat Newcastle United 3-0 in the 1974 FA Cup final.

item.title: States get N2.37 trillion VAT in six months under new sharing formula
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: State governments received N2.37 trillion from Value Added Tax (VAT) allocations in the first half of 2026, up by 23.5% from N1.92 trillion in the corresponding period of 2025. The post States get N2.37 trillion VAT in six months under new sharing formula appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Ex-England manager, Kevin Keegan dies at 75
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former England player and manager, Kevin Keegan, has died at the age of 75. Earlier in January 2026, it was revealed that Keegan had been diagnosed with stage four cancer. His family announced at the time that he was set to begin treatment. Last month, Keegan, while speaking at an event in Newcastle

item.dc:creator: Tribune

item.title: The AirPods Max 2 are down to their second-best price
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: There are a ton of deals available to shop today thanks to Best Buy’s “Black Friday in July” sale lasting all week. The sale typically aligns closely with Prime Day each year, but of course, Amazon’s shopping holiday happened much earlier than we expected. So it’s nice to have some deals in July. On

item.title: Belgium To End Coach Contract Over W/Cup Performance
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Belgium manager Rudi Garcia will leave his role after the country’s football association decided against extending his contract. Garcia led Belgium to the quarter-finals of the 2026 World Cup where they were defeated 2-1 by eventual champions Spain. The 62-year-old replaced Domenico Tedesco as

item.title: Reps launch probe into Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council over legal status, budget inclusion
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Gift ChapiOdekina, Abuja The House of Representatives has inaugurated an ad hoc committee to investigate the legal basis for the establishment and operations of the Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC), including the circumstances surrounding its inclusion in the Federal Gove

item.title: Yamal makes history as first teenager to win Euros, World Cup
item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Spain winger Lamine Yamal has become the first player under the age of 20 to win both the UEFA European Championship and the FIFA World Cup. The Barcelona star reached this historic feat after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final at the New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday.

item.title: Lionheart (CUB) Nears $400 Million Deal for Venezuela Oil Stake
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Lionheart Capital is close to a $400 million deal for a stake in a Venezuelan oil field, in what would be one of the first major private investments in the country’s oil industry since the US began easing sanctions.

item.title: The hidden cost of borrowing abroad to build in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Opinion
item.description: Foreign capital raised to build in Nigeria, whether a power project or a road, arrives carrying a cost that often read more The hidden cost of borrowing abroad to build in Nigeria

item.title: Newcastle, Liverpool and England legend Keegan dies
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Former England captain and manager Kevin Keegan dies at the age of 75, seven weeks after he revealed he had been diagnosed with stage four cancer.

item.title: Court Strikes Out Ned Nwoko’s Suit Challenging Okowa’s APC Candidacy
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abuja has struck out a suit filed by Senator Ned Munir Nwoko seeking to nullify the emergence of former Delta State Governor Ifeanyi Okowa as the All Progressives Congress (APC) candidate for the Delta North Senatorial District in the next general election. According to a rep

item.title: What are ‘civilian’ sites in international law and where have US, Iran hit?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US has escalated attacks on Iranian roads, bridges and power plants, and Iran too, has responded.

item.title: South Korean stocks weren’t a big deal in your emerging-markets fund. Artificial intelligence changed that.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Is your emerging-markets fund an accidental bet on AI? Returns for these two ETFs show why it might be.

item.title: Ghana: Opposition financier Wontumi jailed for 20 years over illegal mining
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Ghana has jailed opposition heavyweight Chairman Wontumi for 20 years over illegal mining, marking the country’s most significant political conviction in the fight against galamsey.

item.title: What we know about US soldiers killed in Iran war, and Iranian casualties
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Trump has expressed sadness and vowed to hit Iran 'very hard' to honour killed US soldiers.

item.title: Prize money FIFA will pay Spain, Argentina after World Cup final revealed
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The prize money Spain and Argentina will receive from FIFA following the World Cup final has been revealed. Recall that Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in the final in New Jersey to lift the 2026 World Cup. Barcelona forward Ferran Torres scored the game’s only goal in extra time. According to ND

item.title: BlackRock Eyes More Than $12 Billion Debt for Texas Data Center
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: BlackRock Inc. is looking to sell more than $12 billion of bonds to help finance a data center campus in El Paso, Texas, according to people with knowledge of the transaction, the latest in a string of debt deals helping to fund tech companies’ massive investments in artificial intelligence.

item.title: NCA Alleges Disparity In N8.05bn Religious Projects In 2026 Bill
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Alleges discrimination, preferential treatment The Northern Christian Association (NCA) has challenged the Federal Government to explain what it described as the glaring disparity in the allocation of about ₦8.05 billion for church and mosque projects in the 2026 Appropriation Bill, saying th
